steps.title: Find what both authors actually say about Asimov's characters
steps.content:
Text 1 says Asimov's characters are "flat and discuss ideas rather than emotions." Text 2 says Foundation "consists of people discussing science and politics" with little revealed about "characters' lives." Both authors independently make the same factual observation: Asimov's characters engage with intellectual topics, not personal or emotional ones. That shared observation is the signal to match.

Now test each choice:
  • Choice A — The Naked Sun is more representative of Asimov's writing style than Foundation is.: neither text compares these two works to each other. ✗
  • Choice B — Asimov's writing style is often unfairly criticized.: Text 1 calls his style "mediocre" and accepts it as a real flaw; Text 2 says the criticism is "wrong." They disagree on whether the criticism is fair. ✗
  • Choice C — Asimov's characters can be concerned mainly with intellectual matters rather than emotions.: both texts state this directly and without dispute. ✓
  • Choice D — Asimov was correct to suggest that individual humans have little influence over the course of human history.: Text 2 describes this as Asimov's thematic intent, but neither author endorses it as true; Text 1 doesn't address it at all. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and close
steps.content:
The correct answer is the one point both authors would agree on: Asimov's characters focus on ideas and intellectual discussion rather than personal emotions — a fact both texts state, regardless of how they evaluate it.

That confirms C is correct.
